concepts.title: Coordinate of the point of contact
concepts.conceptBody:
Point of contact

The coordinate of the point of contact are $\left( \pm \frac{{{a}^{2}}m}{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}}},\mp \frac{m{{b}^{2}}}{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}}} \right)$

concepts.title: Equation of Normals
concepts.conceptBody:
Equation of normal

a) Point Form: The equation of the normal to the hyperbola $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}-\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ at the point

$\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ is }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}-\frac{{{\text{y}}^{2}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{2}}}=1\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ is }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\frac{{{\text{a}}^{2}}\text{x}}{{{\text{x}}_{1}}}+\frac{{{\text{b}}^{2}}\text{y}}{{{\text{y}}_{1}}}={{\text{a}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}$

(b) Parametric Form: The equation of the normal to the hyperbola $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}-\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ at the point $\left( \text{asec}\theta ,\text{btan}\theta \right)$ is $\frac{\text{ax}}{\text{sec}\theta }+\frac{\text{by}}{\text{tan}\theta }={{\text{a}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}$

(c) Slope Form: The equation of normal to the hyperbola $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}-\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ in terms of slope ' $m$ ' is $y=mx\pm \frac{m\left( {{a}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}} \right)}{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}}}$

concepts.title: Number of Normals
concepts.conceptBody:
Number of normals

In general, four normal can be drawn to a hyperbola from a point in its plane i.e., there are four points on the hyperbola, the normal at which will pass through a given point. These four points are called the co-normal points.

Tangent drawn at any point bisects the angle between the lines joining the point to the foci, whereas normal bisects the supplementary angle between the lines.
